mechanism_narrative: Rising retail sales indicate improving nominal household demand and near-term revenue momentum for listed firms, so positive 3-month RSAFS growth predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:latest RSAFS value 667724.0 as of t-2mo
failure_condition: Reject if positive 3-month RSAFS growth is followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in 50% or fewer walk-forward observations, or if Rank IC is below 0.03 over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate crossing into a tightening phase raises discount rates and lowers equity duration valuations, so increases in FEDFUNDS after a low-rate period predict negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS crosses above 0.50% after being below 0.50% for at least 6 months
evidence_refs: fact:latest FEDFUNDS value 0.33 as of t-1mo
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle from near-zero rates
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations produce nonnegative average 60-day S&P 500 returns, or if negative-signal hit rate is below 55% across at least 30 triggered walk-forward cases.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll expansion combined with a low or falling unemployment rate signals durable labor income growth, supporting consumption and earnings expectations; jointly stronger PAYEMS and lower UNRATE predict positive 20-day S&P 500 returns.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS 3-month change is positive and UNRATE is below 4.0%
evidence_refs: fact:latest PAYEMS value 151314.0 and UNRATE value 3.6 as of t-1mo
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with tight labor markets
failure_condition: Reject if the joint PAYEMS-positive and UNRATE-below-4% signal has lower average 20-day returns than unconditional returns, or if its 20-day directional accuracy is below 52% in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production weakness signals deteriorating goods-sector output and operating leverage, so a sustained downside break in INDPRO predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO crosses below its 6-month moving average after two consecutive monthly declines
evidence_refs: fact:latest INDPRO value 104.5853 as of t-2mo
failure_condition: Reject if triggered INDPRO downside breaks are followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 55% of cases, or if mean triggered returns exceed unconditional mean returns.

mechanism_narrative: High housing starts under low short rates create a construction and durable-goods demand impulse without immediate financing stress, so elevated HOUST combined with low FEDFUNDS predicts positive 60-day S&P 500 returns.
candidate_trigger: HOUST is above its 12-month moving average and FEDFUNDS remains below 1.00%
evidence_refs: fact:latest HOUST value 1793.0 as of t-2mo and FEDFUNDS value 0.33 as of t-1mo
failure_condition: Reject if the joint HOUST-above-trend and FEDFUNDS-below-1% condition does not deliver higher 60-day average returns than either single condition alone in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating CPI while FEDFUNDS remains very low signals negative real rates and future policy catch-up risk, which pressures equity multiples; high CPI momentum with low FEDFUNDS predicts negative 20-day S&P 500 returns.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized growth exceeds 4.0% and FEDFUNDS is below 1.00%
evidence_refs: fact:latest CPIAUCSL value 288.663 as of t-1mo and FEDFUNDS value 0.33 as of t-1mo
regime_hint: inflation acceleration with policy behind the curve
failure_condition: Reject if triggered high-CPI low-FEDFUNDS observations produce positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of cases, or if their mean return is not below the unconditional mean return.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp upward crossing in unemployment signals rising recession risk and earnings downgrades before quarterly activity data confirm it, so a rapid UNRATE increase predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by at least 0.30 percentage points over 3 months
evidence_refs: fact:latest UNRATE value 3.6 as of t-1mo
failure_condition: Reject if UNRATE rise triggers have nonnegative average 5-day S&P 500 returns, or if downside directional accuracy is below 55% across triggered observations.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth reflects broad demand and income expansion, so positive quarter-over-quarter GDPC1 growth predicts higher aggregate earnings expectations and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:latest GDPC1 value 19735.895 as of t-4mo
failure_condition: Reject if positive GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter growth is followed by lower 60-day average S&P 500 returns than negative GDPC1 growth, or if rolling 3-year Rank IC is below 0.03.
